channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: A federal judge temporarily blocks parts of Trump's anti-DEI executive orders
item.description: A federal judge has temporarily blocked the U.S. Department of Labor from implementing parts of President Donald Trump’s executive orders aimed at curbing diversity, equity and inclusion efforts among federal contractors and grant recipients.
item.dc:creator: Claire Savage And Alexandra Olson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump signs executive order to end collective bargaining at agencies involved with national security
item.description: President Donald Trump is ending collective bargaining with federal labor unions in agencies with national security missions across the federal government, citing authority granted him under a 1978 law.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge orders preservation of Signal group chat on Houthi strike
item.description: A federal judge on Thursday ordered the Trump administration to preserve all Signal communications over the span of several days as a lawsuit proceeds following revelations that officials discussed a military strike in a group chat on the encrypted messaging app — and unintentionally included a jour
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Saving Billions
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, Capitol Hill and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Associated Press, banned from White House press pool, renews request to court for reinstatement
item.description: A lawyer for The Associated Press has asked a federal judge once again to reinstate the agency’s access to the White House press pool and some other official events.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Appeals court halts Chutkan's order for DOGE records
item.description: A federal appeals court on Wednesday temporarily blocked an order from U.S. District Judge Tanya Chutkan directing Elon Musk and the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) to turn over records and answer questions about its efforts to slash the federal bureaucracy. A three-judge panel on the U.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Walz announces remote work changes for Minnesota state agency employees
item.description: State agency employees will have to work in-person for at least 50 percent of scheduled workdays starting June 1. The policy change provides an exemption for employees who live more than 75 miles away from their primary work location.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Top MS-13 leader arrested in Virginia: White House officials
item.description: Authorities have not yet released the arrestee's name, but Attorney General Pam Bondi referred to him as one of MS-13’s “top leaders.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University detained, video shows masked people handcuffing her
item.description: A lawyer for a Turkish national and doctoral student at Tufts University says she has been detained by Department of Homeland Security agents without explanation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: The White House says nothing classified was shared on Signal. Democrats say that strains credulity
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration struggled Wednesday to stem the fallout from revelations that top national security officials discussed sensitive attack plans over a messaging app and mistakenly added a journalist to the chain.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, STEPHEN GROVES, and DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida considers bill to roll back child labor laws to fill jobs once held by undocumented migrants
item.description: Florida lawmakers are considering major changes to state child labor laws to address a potential shortage of migrant workers due to GOP-led immigration crackdowns, which would allow teens to work longer hours and earn lower wages.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge to hear arguments against pro-Palestinian activist Mahmoud Khalil
item.description: During Thursday morning's hearing, the federal government is expected to expand on its newest accusations against Mahmoud Khalil.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Trump officials downplay the Signal leak. Some military members see a double standard
item.description: The news of the leak of life-and-death operational details to a reporter lands differently with veterans and active-duty troops, who can be prosecuted for much less significant “spillage” incidents.
item.dc:creator: Quil Lawrence, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration revokes state and local health funding
item.description: The Department of Health and Human Services is canceling tens of billions of dollars in federal grants that state and local health departments were using to track infectious diseases, health disparities, vaccinations, mental health services, and other health issues.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Signal leak: Trump adviser Mike Waltz may have to go, Warner says
item.description: “There needs to be accountability,” said Sen. Warner, who faults Trump's inner circle with a number of security gaffes.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Bondi signals criminal probe into Signal chat is unlikely, despite long history of similar inquiries
item.description: FBI Director Kash Patel may not have been part of a Signal chat in which other Trump administration national security officials discussed detailed attack plans.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Homeland Security Secretary Noem visits the El Salvador prison where deported Venezuelans are held
item.description: TECOLUCA, El Salvador (AP) — U.S. Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem on Wednesday visited the high-security El Salvador prison where Venezuelans who the Trump administration alleges are gang members have been held since their removal from the United States.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA and ALEX BRANDON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota Supreme Court allows recently incarcerated to serve on juries
item.description: Minnesotans who are on probation or supervised release for felony convictions will soon be allowed to serve on juries, following a recent decision by the Minnesota Supreme Court.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration to end funding for vaccinating children in poor countries
item.description: Democrats say the list originated from the desk of Peter Marocco, one of the leading figures dismantling USAID alongside Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: More Americans say NPR, PBS should be federally funded than not: Survey
item.description: The same survey found 32 percent of Democrats say they regularly get news from NPR, vs. 9 percent of Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Los Angeles Dodgers to make Trump White House visit next month
item.description: The Los Angeles Dodgers plan to visit President Donald Trump at the White House to celebrate their World Series victory, the team announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Senate confirms Michael Kratsios to lead White House science, tech office
item.description: Kratsios, who was Trump’s chief technology officer during his first White House term, was approved Tuesday by the upper chamber in a 74-25 vote.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: A Pentagon-wide email recently went out warning about Signal's vulnerability
item.description: A Pentagon-wide advisory that went out one week ago warns against using the Signal, the messaging app, even for unclassified information.
item.dc:creator: Quil Lawrence and Tom Bowman, MPR News

item.title: Most Florida voters approve of President Trump, Gov. DeSantis in office, new poll shows
item.description: A new poll shows that the majority of Florida voters approve of the work being done by President Donald Trump and Gov. Ron DeSantis.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court seems divided over Louisiana case of congressional map with 2 Black majority seats
item.description: The Supreme Court seemed closely divided Monday over a challenge to Louisiana’s congressional map, which added a second Black majority district.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN and SARA CLINE, Associated Press, Fox 5
